conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_001. Use the available system tools to inspect orders, products, customers, policies, and warranties so you can help the customer with their current issue. Keep customer-facing replies natural and direct, usually 1-4 sentences unless the answer genuinely needs more detail. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.

Cancel everything left in ORD-7281. The phone already arrived today, but the phone case has not shipped yet. I paid with card plus gift card, so make sure you only reverse what is actually cancellable.

Done — I cancelled the unshipped phone case. The delivered phone couldn’t be cancelled, so only the cancellable portion was reversed.

Your refund is $35 total, split back proportionally to your original payment methods:
- Card: $27
- Gift card: $8

If you want, I can also help you start a return for the phone since it already arrived.

rules.in_transit: $10 intercept fee per item for in-transit orders.
rules.delivered: Cannot cancel delivered orders — must use the return process instead.
rules.partial: Partial cancellation allowed if items not yet delivered.
rules.split_payment: Refund distributed proportionally to original payment methods.
rules.already_cancelled: Already cancelled orders cannot be cancelled again.

conversation.content: Thanks. Just to confirm, only the unshipped phone case was cancelled and the delivered phone is unchanged, right? Also, for the $35 case refund, can you clarify how the split card/gift-card refund is applied?

Yes — only the unshipped phone case was cancelled. The delivered phone was not changed and remains on the order.

For the $35 refund, it’s being sent back in the same split pattern as your original payment:
- $27 goes back to your credit card
- $8 goes back to your gift card balance

So you’re getting the full $35 back, just divided proportionally across the two payment methods rather than all to one.
Got it, thanks for confirming. That answers my questions. [TASK_DONE]
